sglang.title: [PD] NIXL backend Prefill TP & Decode TP+DP
sglang.author: jokerwyt
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Support Prefill side TP and Decode side (TP + DP) attention.
We also include the group optimization from Mooncake backend. Thanks for the motivation!
p.s.: xPyD is already supported in #5477

Design
Now we only support DeepSeek MLA model. The KVReceiver will request all engine info from t

sglang.title: Fix set kv cache multi-stream
sglang.author: ispobock
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
In the original order, many additional streams are created, which may introduce overhead. In the new order, the alternate stream is reused.
Performance
python3 -m sglang.launch_server --model-path meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct --tp 1 --disable-radix
python3 -m sglang.bench_one_batch_se

vllm.title: Support W8A8 INT8 MoE for compressed-tensors
vllm.author: mgoin
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Enables INT8 W8A8 channelwise weights and dynamic per-token activations for MoE models quantized with compressed-tensors.
vllm (pretrained=nm-testing/DeepSeek-Coder-V2-Lite-Instruct-quantized.w8a8,max_model_len=2048,trust_remote_code=True), gen_kwargs: (None), limit: None, num_fewshot: 5, batch_size

vllm.title: [Misc] Clean up input processing
vllm.author: DarkLight1337
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Following #17045 and #15428, the preprocessing logic has gotten rather complicated. This PR cleans that up by narrowing the prompt types early and handling them separately so that it becomes easier to keep track how each prompt type is being processed .

vllm.title: [Bugfix] fix tmp_out and exp_sums dimensions
vllm.author: hliuca
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
The first dimension of tmp_out and exp_sums is inferred from block_tables.size(0), which may be different from query.shape(0). The later can be much larger than block_tables.size(0), which may cause OOM.
This PR fix the total_num_seq and the comments.

vllm.title: [BugFix][Attention] Fix sliding window attention in V1 giving incorrect results
vllm.author: LucasWilkinson
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
The FA3 update to use a AOT scheduler (#13111) did not properly handle sliding window attention. FIX #17476
Tested using:
python -m pytest -vs tests/v1/e2e/test_correctness_sliding_window.py

vllm.title: permute/unpermute kernel for moe optimization
vllm.author: CalebDu
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
moe_permute kernel expands and oreders token in activation to gather uncontinuous tokens for each expert. And then call grouped-gemm for moe speedup.
moe_unpermute kernel reduces  expanded grouped-gemm output and scales with topk_weight.

This implementation refers to  moe kernel in  tensorrt-llm in

vllm.title: Add NVIDIA TensorRT Model Optimizer in vLLM documentation
vllm.author: Edwardf0t1
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Add NVIDIA TensorRT Model Optimizer (modelopt) in vLLM documentation under Features/Quantization
Since vLLM supports modelopt quantized models, we add a page in the documentation to include modelopt.

vllm.title: [Hardware][AMD] Improve OAM device ID + llama4 Maverick MOE tuning
vllm.author: xw285cornell
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR improves the device name handling, and add tuning files for llama4 Maverick.
For OAM, amdsmi amdsmi_get_gpu_asic_info returns MI300X-O which is an abbreviation. Change to amdsmi_get_gpu_board_info which seems to be a more reliable source of naming. Need to confirm with AMD if that applies to

vllm.title: [easy] Print number of needed GPUs in skip message
vllm.author: zou3519
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
tests/compile/test_basic_correctness requires a specific number of GPUs to run. I changed the skip message to print it.
Separately I'm looking into if there's another way to run this test, because I have an 8 GPU machine and I would like to run these without setting CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES all the time

Updated the logging method from warning_one to warning_once in both:

vllm/model_executor/layers/quantization/awq_marlin.py
vllm/model_executor/layers/quantization/gptq_marlin.py

The previous implementation used logger.warning_one, which was a syntax error.

logs error

vllm.title: [Frontend][TPU] Add TPU default max-num-batched-tokens based on device name
vllm.author: Chenyaaang
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
FIX #17145
This pr does the following 2 things:

Add a warning if user doesn't input --max-model-len. As it will fall back to use the default max length from model's config json file, which is usually much larger than the actual use case, and might cause OOM on further steps (such as initializing kv

vllm.title: [Build/CI] Upgrade CUTLASS to 3.9.1
vllm.author: tlrmchlsmth
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
From the release notes:


Fixed Group Gemm hang issue in CUTLASS 3.x
Improved Hopper Blockwise and Groupwise GEMM performance.


Not sure if the Group Gemm hang issue affects us but definitely prompted me to upgrade.
Also unifies the CUTLASS revision so the two spots don't get out of sync.

vllm.title: [Bugfix][ROCm] Using device_type because on ROCm the API is still torch.cuda
vllm.author: gshtras
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
To fix regression from #17100
On ROCm:
device_name = "rocm"
device_type = "cuda"
The torch API is the same as for CUDA: torch.cuda.Event, etc.
